skill.name: clojure-review
skill.description: Review Clojure and ClojureScript changes against Metabase style, module, API, testing, and readability standards.
skill.summary: Review Metabase Clojure and ClojureScript diffs for style and quality issues.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and keyword patterns, but review shows they are markdown code spans, examples, paths, and style-guide text. The confirmed risk is legitimate local command execution through Bash for linting, formatting, tests, REPL evaluation, and optional commits; no malicious intent, prompt injection, network exfiltration, or credential handling was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and instructs assistants to run repository-local commands such as linting, formatting, tests, readability checks, and REPL evaluation. These commands are appropriate for Clojure review, but they can execute project scripts and should be used only in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools header explicitly includes Bash, and the guide provides concrete local commands. The commands are not malicious, but they create normal execution risk in untrusted workspaces.

low_findings.title: Static Command Pattern Alerts Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The scanner reported many Ruby or shell backtick findings, but the referenced lines are markdown inline code spans, style examples, file paths, checklist items, and documented local commands. This is primarily a false positive pattern match, with the real concern covered by the local command execution finding.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are prose, inline code examples, or checklist entries. They are not Ruby backtick execution sites or executable source code.

low_findings.title: False Positive Crypto, Reconnaissance, and Hard-Link Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and hard-link findings are not supported by the reviewed content. The cited lines contain the skill description, style-guide prose, Clojure examples, command output labels, or a quoted REPL example rather than cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, or hard-link creation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations do not contain weak cryptographic algorithms, host reconnaissance behavior, or hard-link commands. The alerts appear to be substring matches inside ordinary markdown and examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash as an allowed tool, which enables command execution during review workflows.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools front matter directly lists Bash. This is a capability risk, not evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes local commands for mage linting, formatting, testing, readability checks, and REPL evaluation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and tied to normal development tasks. No external network endpoint or hidden payload was found.

content.value_statement: Clojure pull requests can miss project-specific style, API, module, and testing rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused Metabase Clojure review checks.
actual_capabilities: Checks Clojure and ClojureScript diffs against Metabase naming and style rules.
Guides use of mage commands for kondo linting, formatting, tests, and readability checks.
Reviews REST API endpoints for schemas, parameter naming, route naming, and test coverage.
Flags module organization, Toucan model, MBQL, driver, and settings convention issues.
Provides focused review comment wording for style violations and potential issues.
Encourages concise review output without approval-only comments.
limitations: The guidance is specific to Metabase conventions and may not fit other Clojure projects.
It can run local repository commands when Bash is available, so untrusted repositories need caution.
It does not replace human judgment for architecture, product behavior, or security design.
It depends on project tools such as mage, clj-kondo, cljfmt, tests, or REPL access being available.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Style Review
use_cases.description: Review changed Clojure files for naming, docstring, privacy, formatting, and test convention issues before merge.

use_cases.title: API Endpoint Review
use_cases.description: Check new REST endpoints for response schemas, Malli parameter schemas, route naming, and required tests.

use_cases.title: Module Convention Audit
use_cases.description: Verify module namespaces, public API placement, model organization, settings, schemas, and driver guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Diff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Clojure diff with the clojure-review skill. Focus on Metabase style violations and missing tests.

prompt_templates.title: Check One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of a changed namespace
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Clojure file for naming, docstrings, privacy markers, function size, and formatting conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit API Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these REST API changes for Metabase endpoint conventions, Malli schemas, response schemas, route naming, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Run Full Review Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the clojure-review workflow on this branch. Run the relevant mage lint, format, readability, and test checks, then summarize only actionable issues.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a public Clojure function in src without a docstring.
output_examples.output: This public var needs a docstring explaining its purpose, inputs, and outputs.

output_examples.input: A route adds a new endpoint without a response schema or tests.
output_examples.output: This endpoint needs a response schema and tests. New Metabase REST API endpoints must define both.

output_examples.input: A helper function is only used inside one namespace but is public.
output_examples.output: This function is only used in this namespace, so it should be marked private.

best_practices: Run linting once at the start and use the results to guide targeted review.
Leave comments only for violations or risks that need action from the author.
Check tests, API schemas, naming, namespace layout, and readability together for complete review coverage.
anti_patterns: Do not leave approval-only comments that say code follows the style guide.
Do not use the skill outside Metabase-style Clojure projects without adapting the rules.
Do not run local Bash commands in untrusted repositories without reviewing what project scripts execute.

faq.question: What languages does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews Clojure and ClojureScript changes using Metabase coding standards.

faq.question: Can it run linters and tests?
faq.answer: Yes. When Bash is available, it guides use of mage commands for kondo, formatting, readability, and tests.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-Metabase projects?
faq.answer: It may help, but many rules are specific to the Metabase repository and its conventions.

faq.question: Does it automatically fix code?
faq.answer: It can suggest or apply obvious fixes when the assistant has edit access, but review comments remain the primary workflow.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill permits local Bash commands for development tooling. That is useful but requires trust in the repository scripts.

faq.question: What should reviewers avoid?
faq.answer: Reviewers should avoid praise-only comments and should focus on actionable violations or potential issues.
